name: Mike Schmidt
notes: Night guard and player character of FNAF 1; fired after seven nights for tampering with the animatronics.
Popular theory identifies him as Michael Afton using an alias; treat as theory, not fact.
The movie's Mike Schmidt is cataloged as mike-schmidt-movie.

name: Phone Guy

status.source: Five Nights at Freddy's 1 (Night 4 phone call is cut off by animatronic sounds)

notes: Voiced by Scott Cawthon.
His FNAF 2 and FNAF 3 appearances are voice recordings set earlier in the in-universe timeline.

name: William Afton

status.source: Five Nights at Freddy's 3 minigames (springlock failure while hiding from the children's spirits)

notes: The franchise's main antagonist: co-founder of Fazbear Entertainment and the missing children's murderer, depicted as the 'Purple Guy' in minigames.
Continues as springtrap, scraptrap and (disputedly) glitchtrap and burntrap — see those entries.
Father of Michael, Elizabeth and (by strong implication) the Crying Child.

name: Michael Afton

status.source: Sister Location Custom Night cutscenes (Ennard used his body as a disguise before abandoning it)

notes: Sister Location's protagonist, sent by his father to 'put his sister back together'.
Strongly implied to be Freddy Fazbear's Pizzeria Simulator's playable employee, dying in its fire.
The popular theory that FNAF 1's Mike Schmidt is Michael under an alias is tracked in the mike-schmidt entry as theory.

name: Elizabeth Afton

status.value: Deceased; soul bound to Circus Baby
status.source: Sister Location (killed by Circus Baby's claw, narrated in Baby's ice cream story)

notes: William Afton's daughter; see circus-baby and scrap-baby for her animatronic vessels.

name: Crying Child

status.source: Five Nights at Freddy's 4 (victim of the Bite of '83 during his birthday party)

notes: FNAF 4's playable child, strongly implied to be William Afton's youngest son.
His real name is unconfirmed; community theories (e.g. 'Evan', 'C.C.') remain speculation.
Theories connecting him to Golden Freddy's second soul are tracked as theory only.

name: Henry Emily

status.source: Freddy Fazbear's Pizzeria Simulator (dies in the fire he set to end the franchise's horrors)

notes: Co-founder of Fazbear Entertainment and original animatronic creator, known as 'Cassette Man' until named in later materials.
Father of Charlotte Emily; orchestrated Freddy Fazbear's Pizzeria Simulator as a trap for the possessed animatronics.

name: Charlotte Emily

status.value: Deceased; soul bound to the Puppet
status.source: Freddy Fazbear's Pizzeria Simulator (Security Puppet minigame)

notes: Henry Emily's daughter and William Afton's first victim, locked outside the restaurant in the rain.

name: Jeremy Fitzgerald
notes: FNAF 2's night guard, moved to the day shift for the 1987 birthday party.
Popular theory makes him the Bite of '87 victim; tracked as theory.
Not to be confused with 'Jeremy', the name attributed to Bonnie's soul.

name: Fritz Smith
notes: FNAF 2's Night 7 guard, fired the same day for tampering with the animatronics and odor.
Not to be confused with 'Fritz', the name attributed to Foxy's soul.

name: Gregory
notes: Security Breach's protagonist, trapped overnight in the Mega Pizzaplex; guides Cassie by walkie-talkie in RUIN.
The 'GGY' story in Tales from the Pizzaplex #5 fuels theories connecting him to the Mimic and Vanny; tracked as theory.

name: Vanessa
notes: The Mega Pizzaplex's night security guard.
Her alter ego as Vanny is cataloged separately; see vanny.
Verify whether her RUIN and Help Wanted 2 appearances warrant adding those ids.

name: Vanny
notes: Vanessa's alter ego under Glitchtrap's influence: the 'Reluctant Follower' in a handmade rabbit suit.
Debuted as a voice/silhouette cameo in Help Wanted before appearing physically in Security Breach.
Using variantOf for a human alter ego is an editorial decision — same person, two catalog identities.

name: Cassie
notes: RUIN's protagonist, entering the ruined Pizzaplex to rescue her friend Gregory.
Verify her connections to Help Wanted 2 (her father is tied to the Mask) before adding fnaf-hw2.

name: William Afton (Novel Trilogy)

status.value: Springlocked inside the Spring Bonnie suit; deteriorates across the trilogy
status.source: The Silver Eyes through The Fourth Closet

notes: Operates under the alias Dave Miller; the novels' counterpart of the games' Springtrap arc.

name: Charlie (Novel Trilogy)

status.value: Revealed to be an android created by Henry

notes: Protagonist of the novel trilogy; her true nature is the trilogy's central twist.
Type kept as Human to avoid spoiling queries; the reveal is documented in status.

name: Henry (Novel Trilogy)
notes: Charlie's father and the animatronics' creator; his legacy drives the trilogy's plot.
Verify appearances in The Twisted Ones before publishing.

name: Mike Schmidt (Movie)
notes: Played by Josh Hutcherson; guardian of his younger sister Abby, haunted by his brother Garrett's abduction.
Unlike the games, the movie's Mike has no established connection to the Afton family.

name: Abby Schmidt
notes: Played by Piper Rubio; Mike's younger sister, able to communicate with the ghost children.

name: Vanessa Shelly (Movie)

status.value: Hospitalized after being stabbed by her father

notes: Played by Elizabeth Lail; police officer revealed to be William Afton's daughter — a major divergence from the games' Vanessa.
Verify her status and role in Five Nights at Freddy's 2 (2025) before publishing.

name: William Afton (Movie)

status.value: Springlocked inside the Yellow Rabbit suit

notes: Played by Matthew Lillard; operates under the alias Steve Raglan, a career counselor.
His animatronic form is cataloged as yellow-rabbit-movie.
Verify the details of his return in Five Nights at Freddy's 2 (2025) before publishing.

name: John (Novel Trilogy)
notes: Charlie's closest friend and love interest; effectively the trilogy's second protagonist by The Fourth Closet.

name: Jessica (Novel Trilogy)
notes: Charlie's childhood friend; part of the returning group across all three novels.

name: Carlton Burke
notes: Comic relief of the friend group; survives being trapped in a springlock suit in The Silver Eyes and a remnant injection in The Fourth Closet.

name: Clay Burke
notes: Carlton's father and Hurricane's police chief; one of the few adults who believes the group.
Verify the extent of his role in The Twisted Ones before publishing.

name: Sammy Emily
notes: Charlie's twin, believed for most of the trilogy to be the child William abducted from Fredbear's Family Diner.
The Fourth Closet inverts this belief — the abducted twin was Charlie herself; documented at claim level in charlie-novels.
Appears mostly through flashbacks and the finale; verify his exact page presence before publishing.

name: Michael Brooks

status.value: Deceased; spirit tied to the golden Freddy suit
status.source: The Silver Eyes (leader of the missing children's spirits)

notes: Carlton's childhood friend and one of William's victims; the novels' counterpart to the games' Golden Freddy child debate.
